title: Biology Paper 3 Practical Test - Mark Scheme

questions.question: The tissue of a peeled potato tuber is composed of many similar cells. You are going to investigate the effect of varying concentrations of sucrose solution on some potato tissue.

parts.question: Record the time. After the strips have been in solutions A, B, C and D for 25–35 minutes, pour away the solutions. Remove the strips and place them on a white tile, making sure that you can identify each strip. Record the time. Calculate how long the strips had been left in the solutions, to the nearest minute.
parts.answer: start and end times recorded; between 25–35 minutes + agrees with times given

parts.question: Describe what you did to make sure that, when you removed the strips from the test-tubes, you knew which potato strip had been in which solution.
parts.answer: labelled tile / surface they were placed on; place in labelled container

parts.question: Measure the lengths of strips A, B, C and D. Record the lengths of each strip at the start and end of the investigation and calculate the changes in length. Enter your results in the table.
parts.answer: all start lengths entered + all 80; all end lengths recorded; A length > C length; B and D lengths between A and C; change in length all correctly calculated and with ± to indicate gain or loss

parts.question: Gently bend each strip to see how flexible it is. The more easily it bends, the more flexible it is. Decide the order of decreasing flexibility and use the scale to record this in the table. Record the most flexible strip as ++++ and the least flexible as +.
parts.answer: all flexibilities recorded using symbols from key; A least flexible + C most flexible

parts.question: Describe and explain the changes in length and flexibility in the potato strip that had been in solution A.
parts.answer: strip longer; less / least flexible; water moved in / correct reference to osmosis; cells larger / more turgid or increase in turgor pressure

parts.question: Identify two problems with the method used in this investigation that might have led to unreliable conclusions. Suggest a way in which each could have been improved to make the results more reliable.
parts.answer: problem improvement: difficult to cut correct sizes / make small measurements; use corer / chipper / use sharper or finer blade; not much change in lengths; more time / longer strips; only one strip assessed / experiment only done once; use more strips / repeat + calculate mean; flexibility subjective / difficult to assess; hang masses on strip / leave for longer; time in solutions different; time each strip separately; potato strips floated / not fully immersed in solution; weigh down potato strips

parts.question: State two factors, apart from the mass of tissue, that should have been kept constant when using this method.
parts.answer: temperature; volume of solutions; time + investigation / pieces of potato in solution; same potato / variety of potato; surface area (of potato)

parts.question: When he removed the pieces of tissue from the test-tubes, he carefully dried them on a paper towel. Explain why it was important to dry them.
parts.answer: remove excess / surface water or solution; only mass of potato tissue weighed

parts.question: Construct a line graph of the data in the table on the grid below. Join your points with ruled, straight lines.
parts.answer: sucrose on x-axis + fully labelled with units; linear scales + origin labelled + good use of grid; all points plotted correctly; straight lines connecting plotted points + touching each point

parts.question: Use your graph to determine the concentration of sucrose which will produce no change in mass.
Describe the method you used to determine this concentration of sucrose.
parts.answer: reference to original mass of 5 g as point to interpolate line from y-axis

questions.question: The photomicrograph shows the central part of a plant root as seen under a light microscope.

parts.question: The xylem and phloem are surrounded by a layer of tissue that is one cell thick. The cell labelled E is in this layer. Make a large drawing of cell E and the cells that touch it on the outside and inside of the layer.
parts.answer: 5 cm minimum size; outline of cell E drawn with sharp pencil + no shading anywhere; cell E with inner and lateral walls thick; cell E + only two cells on inside and two cells on outside + both outside cells larger than inside cells

parts.question: Measure the diameter of the xylem vessel between F and G.
Calculate the actual diameter of the xylem vessel.

Some of the outer cells in the photomicrograph contain small particles. Describe in detail how you would prepare a section of the root for observation under the microscope to see whether these cells contain starch.
parts.answer: cut thin slice of root; place on (microscope) slide; stain section with iodine; add coverslip + prevent bubbles; place slide on microscope + focus lens; stains black if starch present
